sections.title: Talking to King Vargas before the quest

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your Majesty.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: That fool Sigrid! What does she think she's doing?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Excuse me, your Majesty?

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: Ah, [Fremennik name]. I apologise, but I'm rather busy at the moment.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's going on, your Majesty?

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: I'm busy, [Fremennik name]. You'd better talk to Ghrim.

sections.title: Talking to Queen Sigrid before the quest

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your Majesty...

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: Vargas, you irritating fool. 'Warmongering harpy', indeed!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry?

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: Oh, I apologise. I was merely thinking out loud.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How is your kingdom faring?

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: It could be better. Now, if you don't mind, I'm rather busy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Very well, your Majesty.

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: Greetings, Your Royal Highness.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What am I meant to be doing again?

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: Dealing with all the little troubles that come up from time to time, Your Royal Highness.

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: Such as the predicament we are now in.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Has anything been happening in the kingdom recently?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Has anything been happening in the kingdom recently?

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: Interesting that you should ask that, Your Royal Highness.

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: You see, the people are worried that war will break out again.

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: King Vargas has found himself with little to do now that you are the regent. He intended, at first, to return to adventuring.

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: Unfortunately, we still have not had the doors to the throne room enlarged so that he can leave...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, what has he been doing?

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: He has been trying to rule once more, in your absence. However, he has once again quarrelled with the Queen of Etceteria.

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: The two of them have been writing letters to each other, threatening to raise armies and declare war on each other once more!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see.

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: Your Royal Highness, I suggest that you deal with this matter and reconcile the two of them again. Such a quarrel is not good for Miscellania.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Very well, I'll sort it out.

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: Thank you, Your Royal Highness.

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: I suggest you talk to the prince and princess to find out more about the situation.

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: Princess Astrid has been asking after you. She would very much like to see you once again.

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: Prince Brand has been asking after you. He would very much like to see you once again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I don't have the time at the moment.

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: You are the regent of this kingdom, Your Royal Highness. I would urge you to reconsider.

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: However, you are also a busy hero, and thus I understand your reluctance.

sections.title: Talking to King Vargas after starting the quest

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your Majesty.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: I'm sorry, [Fremennik name], but I'm very busy. Talk to my son and daughter if Ghrim can't help you.

sections.title: Talking to Advisor Ghrim after starting the quest

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: Greetings, Your Royal Highness.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What am I meant to be doing again?

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: Talking to the Prince and Princess, Your Royal Highness. I believe they have something to ask you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good day, your Highness.

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: Good day, [Fremennik name]. Have you come to see my sister?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, Advisor Ghrim said I should talk to both of you about recent events in the kingdom.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, dear.

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: Welcome back, dear!

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: I'm afraid I haven't managed to find a priest for our marriage yet.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's a pity.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, Advisor Ghrim said I should talk to you and the Princess about recent events in the kingdom.

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: I'll ask Father if he minds us discussing this in the throne room for a while.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Advisor Ghrim told me to talk to you about the situation in Miscellania.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What has happened while I've been away?

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: It's terrible, my dear! My father's been arguing with the Queen of Etceteria, and has managed to start a war again.

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: It's awful that your work has been undone by such an accident.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean, 'an accident'?

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: Well, if my father had had something to occupy him, he wouldn't have gotten bored and decided to argue with Queen Sigrid again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What would you suggest he do, then?

lines.speaker: Princess Astrid
lines.text: We can't have him declaring war all the time. Even if it would give us a chance to show off our military might.

lines.speaker: Princess Astrid
lines.text: And I could finally show him that archery isn't a cowardly skill. If only we had an army, we could prove ourselves against the Etceterians.

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: Astrid, I have an even better idea.

lines.speaker: Princess Astrid
lines.text: Better than archery? What is it?

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: I think we should persuade our father and Queen Sigrid to marry!

lines.speaker: Princess Astrid
lines.text: ...What are you talking about, Brand?

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: Think of the poetic irony of the situation!

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: The best part is that I could write their wedding hymn for them.

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: 'Today is a happy day, we are getting married,

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: Our national anthems are wonderful, won't you sing this song?'

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure that's such a good idea...

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: I think it will work.

lines.speaker: Princess Astrid
lines.text: I quite agree!

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: Perhaps it's an odd idea, my dear, but since we don't have an army, we can hardly declare war...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you sure it'll work?

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: Of course it will! It always happens in the ballads and stories.

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: Two people who start out hating each other, and end up falling in love. It's a classic tale.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That doesn't always work...

lines.speaker: Princess Astrid
lines.text: But we don't have an army, so it's our best hope.

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: So, will you talk to our father and Queen Sigrid, then?

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: I'm sure you can manage to arrange it all.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll give it a try. But they'll still be angry with each other, so I don't think it'll work.

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: I'm sure you'll manage to do it, dear.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good day, your Highness.

lines.speaker: Princess Astrid
lines.text: Good day, [Fremennik name]. Have you come to see my brother?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, Advisor Ghrim said I should talk to both of you about recent events in the kingdom.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, dear.

lines.speaker: Princess Astrid
lines.text: Welcome back, dear!

lines.speaker: Princess Astrid
lines.text: I'm afraid I haven't managed to find a priest for our marriage yet.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's a pity.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, Advisor Ghrim said I should talk to you and the Prince about recent events in the kingdom.

lines.speaker: Princess Astrid
lines.text: I'll ask Father if he minds us discussing this in the throne room for a while.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What has happened while I've been away?

lines.speaker: Princess Astrid
lines.text: It's terrible, my [dear/dear friend]! My father's been arguing with the Queen of Etceteria, and has managed to start a war again.

lines.speaker: Princess Astrid
lines.text: It's awful that your work has been undone by such an accident.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean, 'an accident'?

lines.speaker: Princess Astrid
lines.text: Well, if my father had had something to occupy him, he wouldn't have gotten bored and decided to argue with Queen Sigrid again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What would you suggest he do, then?

lines.speaker: Princess Astrid
lines.text: We can't have him declaring war all the time. Even if it would give us a chance to show off our military might.

lines.speaker: Princess Astrid
lines.text: And I could finally show him that archery isn't a cowardly skill. If only we had an army, we could prove ourselves against the Etceterians.

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: Astrid, I have an even better idea.

lines.speaker: Princess Astrid
lines.text: Better than archery? What is it?

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: I think we should persuade our father and Queen Sigrid to marry!

lines.speaker: Princess Astrid
lines.text: ...What are you talking about, Brand?

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: Think of the poetic irony of the situation!

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: The best part is that I could write their wedding hymn for them.

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: 'Today is a happy day, we are getting married,

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: Our national anthems are wonderful, won't you sing this song?'

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure that's such a good idea...

lines.speaker: Princess Astrid
lines.text: I think it will work.

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: I quite agree!

lines.speaker: Princess Astrid
lines.text: Perhaps it's an odd idea, my [dear/dear friend], but since we don't have an army, we can hardly declare war...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you sure it'll work?

lines.speaker: Princess Astrid
lines.text: I trust my brother's judgement.

lines.speaker: Princess Astrid
lines.text: Though his ideas can sometimes be a little... strange.

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: What was that, Astrid?

lines.speaker: Princess Astrid
lines.text: I'm just telling [Fremennik name] about your poetic accomplishments.

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: Oh, that's all right, then.

lines.speaker: Princess Astrid
lines.text: So, will you talk to our father and Queen Sigrid, then?

lines.speaker: Princess Astrid
lines.text: I'm sure you can manage to arrange it all.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll give it a try. But they'll still be angry with each other, so I don't think it'll work.

lines.speaker: Princess Astrid
lines.text: I'm sure you'll manage to do it, [dear/dear friend].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your Majesty.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: What is it? You'll have to excuse me, I'm quite busy...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you busy with, your Majesty?

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: Affairs of state, and so on.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I thought I was supposed to deal with those, once you appointed me Regent.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: Well, there's not all that much for me to do these days, you see.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: And though you do make a fine regent, you cannot be here all the time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you're still helping to rule, your Majesty?

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: In a manner of speaking.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: These 'affairs of state' you're dealing with...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Would these involve starting a war with Queen Sigrid of Etceteria?

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: Not exactly...

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: Well, yes. Of course, Sigrid was the one who insulted me first.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: And that's the least of the kingdom's problems at the moment.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: Some of Frodi's barrels are missing, as are many of Magnus's crates. Leif also says he's had stacks of wood disappear.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: Since you're the regent, you should investigate what has happened to these missing resources.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right away, your majesty.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: I'm glad to see the kingdom in such capable hands.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sorry, I have some other things to do.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: That never prevented me from ruling this kingdom when I was your age, [Fremennik name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I suppose I could spare a little time.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: I'm glad to have your help.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: All you need to do for the moment is to talk to the citizens of Miscellania, and find out what has been happening.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: It might also be useful to interrogate the citizens of Etceteria to see what they know.

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: Have you managed to make peace between my father and Queen Sigrid yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not quite yet.

lines.speaker: Princess Astrid
lines.text: Have you managed to make peace between my father and Queen Sigrid yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not quite yet.

lines.speaker: Gardener Gunnhild
lines.text: Good day, Your Royal Highness.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: King Vargas tells me that things have been going missing from the kingdom. Can you tell me anything about this?

lines.speaker: Gardener Gunnhild
lines.text: You mean you hadn't heard, Your Royal Highness? Etceterian soldiers came and demanded tithes from us.

lines.speaker: Gardener Gunnhild
lines.text: You're the only person we give any of our goods to, so we refused, and found the next day that some of our goods had been stolen.

lines.speaker: Lumberjack Leif
lines.text: Good day, Your Royal Highness.

lines.speaker: Lumberjack Leif
lines.text: You hadn't heard about it, Your Royal Highness? Etceterian soldiers came and demanded tithes from us.

lines.speaker: Lumberjack Leif
lines.text: You're the only person we give any of our goods to, so we refused, and found the next day that some of our goods had been stolen.

lines.speaker: Miner Magnus
lines.text: Good day, Your Royal Highness.

lines.speaker: Miner Magnus
lines.text: You mean you don't know, Your Royal Highness? Etceterian soldiers came and demanded tithes from us.

lines.speaker: Miner Magnus
lines.text: You're the only person we give any of our goods to, so we refused, and found the next day that some of our goods had been stolen.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: Have you spoken to the citizens yet, [Fremennik name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've spoken to the Miscellanean citizens.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: And?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They say that the Etceterian army demanded tithes from them, and took some of their goods even though they refused.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: So, Sigrid's planning to invade, is she?

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: Or perhaps she's claiming that Miscellania is part of Etceteria now!

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: [Fremennik name], go and talk to Sigrid and ask her what she thinks she's doing.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: Exchanging angry letters is understandable, but where did she get an army from?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll talk to her right away, your Majesty.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your Majesty, do you have a moment?

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: Incompetent! I'll give him 'incompetent'...

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: Yes, what is it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I hear from King Vargas that relations between Etceteria and Miscellania are none too cordial at the moment.

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: Of course not!

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: That idiot Vargas has decided to take a hand in ruling Miscellania, even though he'd handed it over to you, and now he's declared war on Etceteria again!

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: I suppose all those insults I made about his parentage can't have helped the matter...

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: Even so, that doesn't excuse what he did to my citizens!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What happened, your Majesty?

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: My citizens have told me that items of theirs have been taken, and I'm sure that Vargas is behind it.

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: Since you're the one who's supposed to be ruling Miscellania, you should sort it out.

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: I'm glad you understand.

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: If you talk to the citizens of Etceteria, they will tell you exactly what happened.

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: I would find out for myself, but the pressures of ruling are immense, and I would never have the time free.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course not, it's not my fault.

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: What sort of Regent of Miscellania do you call yourself?

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: Of course it's your fault. You rule the country now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I suppose you're right...

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: I'm glad you understand.

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: If you talk to the citizens of Etceteria, they will tell you exactly what happened.

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: I would find out for myself, but the pressures of ruling are immense, and I would never have the time free.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Excuse me...

lines.speaker: Matilda
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Queen Sigrid tells me that things have been going missing from the kingdom. Can you tell me anything about this?

lines.speaker: Matilda
lines.text: You haven't heard about that until now?

lines.speaker: Matilda
lines.text: I'm surprised.

lines.speaker: Matilda
lines.text: Well, Miscellanian soldiers came and asked for tribute.

lines.speaker: Matilda
lines.text: When I told them to leave, they came back the next day and stole my hat!

lines.speaker: Matilda
lines.text: It was a wonderful hat. Mithril-reinforced, you see, and with a special splitbark brim to keep the sun off.

lines.speaker: Matilda
lines.text: Could you please get it back for me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll see what I can do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Excuse me...

lines.speaker: Helga
lines.text: Yes, can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Queen Sigrid tells me that things have been going missing from the kingdom. Can you tell me anything about this?

lines.speaker: Helga
lines.text: You're the regent of Miscellania, aren't you?

lines.speaker: Helga
lines.text: I'm surprised you didn't know about your own soldiers demanding tribute from us.

lines.speaker: Helga
lines.text: I told them to go away, but the next day I found that they'd stolen my small shield!

lines.speaker: Helga
lines.text: How am I supposed to defend the house without it?

lines.speaker: Helga
lines.text: And what am I going to use as a fruit bowl in the meantime?

lines.speaker: Helga
lines.text: Please sort out your soldiers, and get my shield back to me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll see what I can do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Excuse me...

lines.speaker: Ashild
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Queen Sigrid tells me that things have been going missing from the kingdom. Can you tell me anything about this?

lines.speaker: Ashild
lines.text: Curse those Miscellanian soldiers!

lines.speaker: Ashild
lines.text: They came and demanded tribute, and even though I told them to go away, they came back at night and stole my farming tools!

lines.speaker: Ashild
lines.text: Without my trowel and fork, I can't do anything to keep the weeds from the farming patch.

lines.speaker: Ashild
lines.text: Please, find my tools again, so I can get back to work.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll see what I can do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Excuse me...

lines.speaker: Moldof
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Queen Sigrid tells me that things have been going missing from the kingdom. Can you tell me anything about this?

lines.speaker: Moldof
lines.text: You didn't know about it?

lines.speaker: Moldof
lines.text: Miscellanian soldiers came here a few days ago, saying they wanted us to pay tribute.

lines.speaker: Moldof
lines.text: Of course, I told them to leave.

lines.speaker: Moldof
lines.text: But in the morning, I found they'd stolen my quiver of arrows!

lines.speaker: Moldof
lines.text: They're not very valuable arrows, but I need them to do my job. Could you get them back for me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll see what I can do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Excuse me...

lines.speaker: Arnor
lines.text: Yes, what is it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Queen Sigrid tells me that things have been going missing from the kingdom. Can you tell me anything about this?

lines.speaker: Arnor
lines.text: You mean you haven't heard?

lines.speaker: Arnor
lines.text: Only a few days ago, we had Miscellanian soldiers come round and ask for tribute.

lines.speaker: Arnor
lines.text: I told them to get lost, of course...

lines.speaker: Arnor
lines.text: ...but when I woke up the next morning, they'd stolen my sword!

lines.speaker: Arnor
lines.text: Could you get it back for me? I doubt it can cut anything, but it once belonged to my great-grandfather.

lines.speaker: Arnor
lines.text: So it's a family heirloom, you see.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll see what I can do.

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: Have you talked to the citizens yet, [Fremennik name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've talked to the Miscellanian citizens as well as the Etceterians.

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: What exactly happened?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Etceterian citizens said that Miscellanian soldiers came to their houses and demanded tribute...

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: Just as I suspected! Vargas, you treacherous snake!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ...and the Miscellanian citizens said that the Etceterian army marched into their fields and demanded tithes!

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: Now I see his plan! Vargas isn't content with simply arguing; he wants to start a war even if it means swindling his own citizens!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your Majest-

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: Etceterian army? There hasn't been one for centuries! These Miscellanian soldiers of his must have disguised themselves.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If I may-

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: And since Vargas shouldn't even be ruling now, it's your responsibility to deal with this.

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: The one who should sort this out is you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I suppose you're right.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Me?

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: It's one of the hazards of ruling a country, [Fremennik name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I suppose you're right.

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: Have you found those stolen items yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet, your Majesty.

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: You'd better hurry and look for them.

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: We don't know what Vargas might be planning next!

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: Have you spoken to the citizens yet, [Fremennik name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've spoken to the Miscellanian citizens, and also the Etceterians.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: What did they say?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Miscellanian citizens said that the Etceterian army marched into their fields and demanded tithes...

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: I see. Sigrid thinks that now I've recognised Etceteria as a sovereign nation, I'll just roll over and play dead when she tries to invade, does she?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ...and the Etceterian citizens said that Miscellanian soldiers came to their houses and demanded tribute!

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: And she's even started spreading rumours among her own citizens! Are there no depths to which she won't sink?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your Majesty...

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: Lying to her own people about there being Miscellanian soldiers... we don't even have an army!

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: Which makes it all the worse, as otherwise I'd be able to invade Etceteria to save the citizens from Sigrid's depravity.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure that would work very well, your Majesty.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: Perhaps not, [Fremennik name].

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: In any case, if there are any 'Miscellanian soldiers', they must have come from somewhere.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: Ask Advisor Ghrim if he knows who they might be, or where they might have come from.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: He's a capable advisor, so I'm sure he'll know about any suspicious people.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll do that, your Majesty.

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: Greetings, Your Royal Highness.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: King Vargas asked me to talk to you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He said you might know more about the missing goods.

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: I'm not sure what you're implying, Your Royal Highness.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... that you could advise me where to look for them?

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: Ah, of course.

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: Assuming that nobody presently living in Miscellania would have taken them,

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: it is of course likely that someone from outside took them.

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: The sailor who brought you here from the mainland would be the first to see any suspicious visitors.

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: I suggest you ask him about recent arrivals to the island.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What am I meant to be doing again?

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: ...I believe the King asked you to talk to me, Your Royal Highness.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, that's right.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm looking for a sailor...

lines.speaker: Sailor
lines.text: You've found one here. Can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The King's advisor said I should talk to you. He said there have been some suspicious people arriving recently.

lines.speaker: Sailor
lines.text: Well, I don't know of any suspicious people... Advisor Ghrim can be rather paranoid about these things sometimes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh. Has anyone visited the island recently, then?

lines.speaker: Sailor
lines.text: Nobody apart from you, and those kids from Rellekka.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What kids from Rellekka?

lines.speaker: Sailor
lines.text: Five of them came over here a few weeks ago. They said they were here to see relatives in the dungeons, and they were visiting from the mainland.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I didn't realise the King kept people in his dungeons.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I didn't know Miscellania even HAD dungeons.

lines.speaker: Sailor
lines.text: They've not been used to keep prisoners for a long time. But since the war ended, we've had more people visiting the island.

lines.speaker: Sailor
lines.text: Some of them decided they wanted to stay, so we've had to find somewhere for them to live...

lines.speaker: Sailor
lines.text: Only the weather on Miscellania is terrible, and nobody could build houses that would stand up to a strong wind. Etceteria is just the same.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you decided to live in the dungeons instead?

lines.speaker: Sailor
lines.text: Well, of course, they had to be enlarged first. There are still some dwarves working there, making more of the dungeons inhabitable before they move over to work in Etceteria.

lines.speaker: Sailor
lines.text: If you're looking for those kids from Rellekka, you'll find them in those dungeons.

lines.speaker: Sailor
lines.text: Why are you surprised about this, though? I thought you'd become the Regent.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still an adventurer, and I can't be everywhere in Gielinor...

sections.title: Attempting to enter the dungeon without the scroll

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Sorry, but nobody's allowed down there by order of the King.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I'm the Regent! I order you to let me go down there.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: You're the Regent? Well, I didn't vote for you.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Sorry, 'Regent', nobody can go into the dungeons without the King's say-so.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What'll it take to convince you that I'm really the Regent?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: If the King came and told me, then I'd believe it.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Or, if he can't come here himself, I suppose a royal proclamation would be fine.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A royal proclamation?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Does he have to shout it from the battlements, or something like that?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: No, just his signature and a statement saying you're really the Regent would do.

sections.title: Asking King Vargas for permission to enter the dungeon

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your Majesty, how do I get into the dungeons?

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: The dungeons?

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: Of course, how could I have forgotten?

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: I'm surprised I didn't tell you when you were appointed Regent.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's okay, the sailor explained it to me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He said that the only new arrivals on the island were some teenagers, who came from Rellekka to visit their relatives.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Those relatives were living in the houses in the dungeons.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: Ah, so you wish to look in the dungeons in case these new arrivals are to blame?

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: I doubt that anyone with Miscellanian relatives would be at fault. However...

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: There is something I need you to do in the dungeons.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: They are still being excavated, and the dwarven architects are finding new caves as they go. There have been reports of odd sightings in these new caves.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: Perhaps they are monsters, waiting to devour unwary Miscellanians...

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: Whatever they are, I want you to look into it.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: You are a member of the royal family, after all, so I know I can rely on your help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course, your Majesty.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: The guard by the dungeon entrance may not recognise you, since he was only hired recently. Here, take this scroll to show that you have access to the dungeons.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: You'll find the entrance by the south-east corner of the palace.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: Have you given that scroll to the guard yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet, your Majesty.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I seem to have lost it.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: Honestly... very well, I'll write you another one.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: Try not to lose it this time.

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: Greetings, Your Royal Highness.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What am I meant to be doing again?

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: Showing the dungeon guard that you have permission to enter, Your Royal Highness.

sections.title: Entering the dungeon with the scroll

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Sorry, but nobody's allowed down there by order of the King.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I'm the Regent! I order you to let me go down there.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: You're the Regent? Well, I didn't vote for you.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Sorry, 'Regent', nobody can go into the dungeons without the King's say-so.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Look, the King gave me a scroll saying I have access to the dungeons. I have it right here.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Let's have a look, then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here you go.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Hmm... all seems to be in order.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: All right, your Highness, you're free to go down there.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Try not to be eaten by monsters.

lines.speaker: Halla
lines.text: Welcome to Miscellania's first clothing store! We sell clothing made especially for Fremenniks and Dwarves.

lines.speaker: Halla
lines.text: Can I help you with anything, Your Royal Highness?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's it like living down here?

lines.speaker: Halla
lines.text: It's very spacious. One of the dwarves said that the caves go on for miles!

lines.speaker: Halla
lines.text: I don't like these rumours I've heard about the other caves, though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What rumours?

lines.speaker: Halla
lines.text: They say there might be monsters in there. You'd best talk to the dwarves who are excavating - they'd know about these stories.

lines.speaker: Osvald
lines.text: Welcome to the Miscellania food store. We've only opened recently.

lines.speaker: Osvald
lines.text: Would you like to buy anything, your Royal Highness?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's it like living down here?

lines.speaker: Osvald
lines.text: The town's growing larger, and business is doing well.

lines.speaker: Osvald
lines.text: I'm worried about the stories I've heard about monsters, though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What stories about monsters?

lines.speaker: Osvald
lines.text: I heard that one of the dwarves saw monsters in the other caves!

lines.speaker: Osvald
lines.text: I don't know more than that, so you'd have to ask the dwarves.

lines.speaker: Finn
lines.text: Can I help you, your Royal Highness?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's it like living down here?

lines.speaker: Finn
lines.text: There's no rain or wind down here.

lines.speaker: Finn
lines.text: Some of the dwarves say that there's water in the other caves, and one of them says he's seen monsters in there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Monsters? What sort of monsters?

lines.speaker: Finn
lines.text: I don't know, sorry. You'd better ask one of the dwarves about it.

lines.speaker: Runa
lines.text: Would you like to try some fine Miscellanian ale, Your Royal Highness?

lines.speaker: Runa
lines.text: Well, I say Miscellanian, but it's actually brewed on the mainland.

lines.speaker: Runa
lines.text: Would you like to try some anyway?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's it like living down here?

lines.speaker: Runa
lines.text: The dwarves living down here spend a lot of time and money in my inn.

lines.speaker: Runa
lines.text: One of them's been in here all day, and only bought one pint.

lines.speaker: Runa
lines.text: Could you ask him to at least order another drink?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll see what I can do.

lines.speaker: Alviss
lines.text: [Good day, sir/Good day, madam/Good day].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you doing down here?

lines.speaker: Alviss
lines.text: I'm waiting for my shift. I'm supposed to be digging once the others have finished.

lines.speaker: Alviss
lines.text: I don't like the sound of these monsters, though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Monsters?

lines.speaker: Alviss
lines.text: I heard that Donal went exploring in the caves, and saw a huge monster.

lines.speaker: Alviss
lines.text: If you want to know more about it, ask Thorodin - he's working on the excavation right now.

lines.speaker: Fullangr
lines.text: [Good day, sir/Good day, madam/Good day].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you doing down here?

lines.speaker: Fullangr
lines.text: I'm working on the digging, of course.

lines.speaker: Fullangr
lines.text: It's a small excavation, so only two of us can work on it at a time.

lines.speaker: Fullangr
lines.text: Thorodin and Donal were working on it earlier, but I hear Donal's run off to hide in the inn.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why is that?

lines.speaker: Fullangr
lines.text: Apparently he saw a monster when he was in the caves.

lines.speaker: Fullangr
lines.text: I'm not scared of monsters - I won't let them stop me from digging!

lines.speaker: Jari
lines.text: [Good day, sir/Good day, madam/Good day].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you doing down here?

lines.speaker: Jari
lines.text: I'm waiting to work on the digging.

lines.speaker: Jari
lines.text: It's the first excavation I've worked on, and I'm looking forward to it.

lines.speaker: Jari
lines.text: I heard there were monsters in the other caves, though. I don't really want to meet them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Monsters?

lines.speaker: Jari
lines.text: Donal saw them when he explored the caves. He's been in the inn ever since.

lines.speaker: Thorodin
lines.text: [Good day, sir/Good day, madam/Good day].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you doing down here?

lines.speaker: Thorodin
lines.text: We're extending the cave so more people can live in it.

lines.speaker: Thorodin
lines.text: These Miscellanians aren't so bad. They appreciate the benefits of living underground.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ...such as?

lines.speaker: Thorodin
lines.text: You don't get rained on, for example.

lines.speaker: Thorodin
lines.text: Have you spoken to Donal?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet, no.

lines.speaker: Thorodin
lines.text: Well, if you want to hear stories about monsters, he's the best one to ask.

sections.title: After talking to Donal about the Giant Sea Snake

lines.speaker: Thorodin
lines.text: [Good day, sir/Good day, madam/Good day].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you doing down here?

lines.speaker: Thorodin
lines.text: We're extending the cave so more people can live in it.

lines.speaker: Thorodin
lines.text: These Miscellanians aren't so bad. They appreciate the benefits of living underground.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ...such as?

lines.speaker: Thorodin
lines.text: Not getting rained on, for example.

lines.speaker: Thorodin
lines.text: Have you spoken to Donal?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have. The monster he talked about could be in the caves nearby.

lines.speaker: Thorodin
lines.text: You'd better get in there and do something about it, then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's not safe. I need to shore up the walls before I can go through.

lines.speaker: Thorodin
lines.text: [Good day, sir/Good day, madam/Good day].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you doing down here?

lines.speaker: Thorodin
lines.text: We're extending the cave so more people can live in it.

lines.speaker: Thorodin
lines.text: These Miscellanians aren't so bad. They appreciate the benefits of living underground.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ...such as?

lines.speaker: Thorodin
lines.text: You don't get rained on, for example.

lines.speaker: Thorodin
lines.text: Of course, there are some problems, like the monsters Donal saw...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Someone saw a monster?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you tell me more? The King asked me to investigate.

lines.speaker: Thorodin
lines.text: One of the other dwarves who was working with us saw it. His name's Donal.

lines.speaker: Thorodin
lines.text: He went further into the caves to plan our excavations, and a while later he came out shaking.

lines.speaker: Thorodin
lines.text: He's been in the inn ever since.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good day.

lines.speaker: Donal
lines.text: What do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you doing down in these caves?

lines.speaker: Donal
lines.text: I'm waiting to work on the excavations...

lines.speaker: Donal
lines.text: ...though I don't really want to start digging again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why not? I thought digging was something dwarves were good at.

lines.speaker: Donal
lines.text: Not with monsters like that one in those caves.

lines.speaker: Donal
lines.text: It had huge teeth and flaming red eyes, and was almost the size of the tunnel!

lines.speaker: Donal
lines.text: If it attacks the town...

lines.speaker: Donal
lines.text: Are you an adventurer, by any chance?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Adventurer and Regent of Miscellania.

lines.speaker: Donal
lines.text: In that case, could you go and deal with that monster?

lines.speaker: Donal
lines.text: I don't want to have to face it again if it attacks the town.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course. Dealing with monsters is what I do best!

lines.speaker: Donal
lines.text: You'll need to get into the other caves first, of course.

lines.speaker: Donal
lines.text: There's a crack in the wall where the others are digging, but the rocks are loose, and can fall on you if you're not careful.

lines.speaker: Donal
lines.text: I can give you a mining prop to keep them from falling, if you give me a minute to find it...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, so all I have to do is get through that wall?

lines.speaker: Donal
lines.text: No, after that the only way you can get further is climbing up onto a high ledge, then crawling through a tunnel.

lines.speaker: Donal
lines.text: But the walls are slippery...

lines.speaker: Donal
lines.text: ...and I think I might have broken the lift when I fell off it on my way back.

lines.speaker: Donal
lines.text: You'll probably need to fix the lift - I doubt you'd be able to climb.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And the monster's behind that tunnel?

lines.speaker: Donal
lines.text: Somewhere behind it. I couldn't get very far, what with the flooding in the tunnel.

lines.speaker: Donal
lines.text: There's some rope and some planks near the broken lift - those would probably come in handy.

lines.speaker: Donal
lines.text: Ah, here's that mining prop. I knew I had it somewhere.

lines.speaker: Donal
lines.text: Here you go. Good luck with fighting that monster.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why bother? It can't get through solid rock.

lines.speaker: Donal
lines.text: That's what I thought, until I saw the sheer size of it. This thing probably MADE the tunnels!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And you're sending me in to deal with it alone?

lines.speaker: Donal
lines.text: I've heard stories from the Miscellanians. If you're the Regent, you must be quite a strong hero.

lines.speaker: Donal
lines.text: So what are you worried about? You probably eat monsters like that for breakfast!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I wouldn't put it quite like that...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I'll help you after all. Now, how do I find this monster?

lines.speaker: Donal
lines.text: What do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Just wondering if you were still here.

lines.speaker: Donal
lines.text: Of course I'm still here.

lines.speaker: Donal
lines.text: I'm not going near that crack in the wall again.

lines.speaker: Donal
lines.text: Rock falls and so on are fine, but sea monsters in caves - never!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've lost that mining prop you gave me...

lines.speaker: Donal
lines.text: Lucky I have a spare, isn't it?

lines.speaker: Donal
lines.text: Here you go. Good luck with fighting that monster.

sections.title: Attempting to squeeze through the crevice before adding the mining prop

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's not safe. I need to shore up the walls before I can go through.

sections.title: Adding a beam to the platform
Using items on the lift after repairing it fully
Entering the tunnel with beams or pulley beams
Using a rope on the rock formation

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can see the embers of a fire up ahead. Has someone else been in these caves?

sections.title: Using a plank on the rocks
Searching the second, third, fourth, and fifth fire remains
Searching fire remains you've already collected the pages from
Trying to enter the crevice without all the diary pages

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I should explore this cave more before I jump through any more cracks in the walls...

lines.speaker: Armod
lines.text: What do we do now?

lines.speaker: Armod
lines.text: If you hadn't got drunk in that inn, Signy, we could have stayed there for longer.

lines.speaker: Signy
lines.text: It wasn't just me who got drunk. You looked like you were about to puke all over the barmaid.

lines.speaker: Hild
lines.text: Reinn threw up anyway.

lines.speaker: Reinn
lines.text: I can't help it, I just have a weak stomach.

lines.speaker: Reinn
lines.text: You're no better, Hild. You said you knew where we were, and now look at us!

lines.speaker: Reinn
lines.text: We're cold, we're hungry and now we're LOST!

lines.speaker: Hild
lines.text: We wouldn't have been so cold if Armod had more diary pages he'd let us use!

lines.speaker: Armod
lines.text: Hey! I have to write down what's happened.

lines.speaker: Armod
lines.text: Otherwise father won't believe me when I tell him.

lines.speaker: Signy
lines.text: If your father listened to what you say, we wouldn't BE here.

lines.speaker: Signy
lines.text: We'd have passed the trials and we'd be proper adults!

lines.speaker: Reinn
lines.text: Besides, it's not like he'll even look at the rubbish you've written in your diary.

lines.speaker: Armod
lines.text: If only my father would come and save us.

lines.speaker: Hild
lines.text: If only SOMEONE would come and save us.

lines.speaker: Armod
lines.text: Someone's coming!

lines.speaker: Signy
lines.text: Quick, let's hide!

lines.speaker: Armod
lines.text: What if it's the monster again? What if it's one of the townspeople?

lines.speaker: Armod
lines.text: What if my father's found out what we've been doing?

lines.speaker: Armod
lines.text: H-hey! Where am I going to hide?

lines.speaker: Hild
lines.text: Shhhh! Just pretend you're part of the scenery, or something!

lines.speaker: Reinn
lines.text: You're almost smart enough to make a good rock.

lines.speaker: Armod
lines.text: You want me to hit you?

lines.speaker: Signy
lines.text: Just act normal, Armod! And hurry!

lines.speaker: Armod
lines.text: O-okay.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Armod
lines.text: Don't bother me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry?

lines.speaker: Armod
lines.text: I said, don't bother me!

lines.speaker: Armod
lines.text: I'm being a rock at the moment.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You can't be a rock. I heard you talking to people earlier.

lines.speaker: Armod
lines.text: What did you hear?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your name's Armod, isn't it? I found your diary.

lines.speaker: Armod
lines.text: What? How did you get it? Give it back!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not until you tell me what's going on.

lines.speaker: Armod
lines.text: Why should I tell you? We're going to do really well and show my father that we're true adults!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What, by starting a war?

lines.speaker: Armod
lines.text: What the-

lines.speaker: Armod
lines.text: Did you read my diary?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, all of it.

lines.speaker: Armod
lines.text: What are you even doing in these caves anyway? Everyone else was scared to come down here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I'm the Regent of Miscellania. The King asked me to take a look-

lines.speaker: Armod
lines.text: You're the REGENT?

lines.speaker: Armod
lines.text: Um, you four, I think you'd better come out now. We're in big trouble.

lines.speaker: Reinn
lines.text: Nice impression of a rock you did there, Armod.

lines.speaker: Signy
lines.text: So you're the adventurer we heard about, then. The one who married the [prince/princess].

lines.speaker: Signy
lines.text: So you're the adventurer we heard about, then. The one who gained the trust of the [prince/princess].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, that's me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And you five are the people mentioned in Armod's diary, right?

lines.speaker: Beigarth
lines.text: Armod, you should have burnt it all.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did you steal those goods from the Etceterians and the Miscellanians?

lines.speaker: Armod
lines.text: It was Beigarth's idea, not mine!

lines.speaker: Armod
lines.text: He said that if we disguised ourselves as soldiers, we could convince both the King and Queen that they were under attack. And it almost worked, too.

lines.speaker: Reinn
lines.text: It would have worked, if it hadn't been for that huge snake!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ...What huge snake?

lines.speaker: Reinn
lines.text: We put the things we'd taken somewhere safe, so we could get them back when we made peace again.

lines.speaker: Reinn
lines.text: Everything was going well, until we got back to that place, and a giant sea snake had moved in!

lines.speaker: Reinn
lines.text: It's made a nest out of all the boxes and barrels.

lines.speaker: Armod
lines.text: I don't care about the plan. If you can get rid of that sea snake, we'll be really grateful to you.

lines.speaker: Armod
lines.text: We can't even get out of these dungeons while it's there.

lines.speaker: Hild
lines.text: I agree with him. I want to go back home.

lines.speaker: Hild
lines.text: It's cold here, we've got no food, and we can't go back to the town.

lines.speaker: Signy
lines.text: Not after we got drunk and then didn't pay our bill.

lines.speaker: Armod
lines.text: So, will you get rid of that sea snake?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course. I was going to do that anyway.

lines.speaker: Armod
lines.text: Thank you so much. You've saved us from the brink of death.

lines.speaker: Armod
lines.text: Could I ask you for one more thing?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Armod
lines.text: PLEASE don't tell my father how badly we messed up.

sections.title: Upon killing the Giant Sea Snake
Leaving the Giant Sea Snake's lair

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Hello down there! Are you all right?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who's that?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: I'm up here, above this hole.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: I've thrown down a rope - you sounded like you were trapped in there.

sections.title: Talking to the guard near the hole

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: I'm surprised you managed to get out of there without breaking any bones.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Still, I can't let you go back down there until the Queen says it's safe.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't suppose you've seen a large box around here, have you?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Oh, so this is yours? You must have dropped it around here.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Seems rather heavy. Take better care of it this time.

sections.title: Trying to go back into the hole

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Wait a minute! You can't go down there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why not? I came up through that hole...

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Yes, and I'm surprised you managed to get out of there without breaking any bones.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Still, I can't let you go back down there until the Queen says it's safe.

sections.title: Talking to Queen Sigrid with the heavy box

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've talked to the Miscellanian citizens as well as the Etceterians.

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: What exactly happened?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Etceterian citizens said that Miscellanian soldiers came to their houses and demanded tribute...

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: Just as I suspected! Vargas, you treacherous snake!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ...and the Miscellanian citizens said that the Etceterian army marched into their fields and demanded tithes!

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: Now I see his plan! Vargas isn't content with simply arguing; he wants to start a war even if it means swindling his own citizens!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your Majest-

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: Etceterian army? There hasn't been one for centuries! These Miscellanian soldiers of his must have disguised themselves.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If I may-

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: If he wants an Etceterian army, then we'll have to raise one!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your Majesty!

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've found out what caused all these problems.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A group of teenagers from Rellekka came to Etceteria to prove themselves as adults.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They said they tried to start a war, which they'd then stop, and be hailed as peacemakers.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They were the ones who disguised themselves as soldiers!

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: Hmm...

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: That explains what the citizens said about armies, I suppose.

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: But what about the items that went missing?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, there was a minor problem involving a sea snake...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I dealt with it, and got the items back.

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: Finally, a ruler of Miscellania who's willing to do something for his neighbours.

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: Do you have the items with you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ...I must have dropped the box on my way out of the dungeons.

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: *sigh* Well, if you ask the guard at the entrance to the dungeon, he might have picked it up.

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: Of course, he won't know what it's for, so you'd better bring it back here once you find it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I shall do, your Majesty.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I have them here.

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: Thank you, [Fremennik name]. You've shown yourself to be an excellent diplomat and warrior - not a combination that comes naturally to many.

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: My citizens would like their items back, of course. One of my guards will deal with the items for you, and you will be provided with a suitable reward.

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: Finally... I have a letter that I would like you to give to Vargas. This war was a mistake, and I've made a formal apology.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll make sure to give it to him, your Majesty.

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: Thank you for all your help, [Fremennik name]. To show our gratitude, the woodcutters and other workers of Etceteria have pledged to add a proportion of what they produce to your treasury.

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: Now that you've finished giving these items back, please give my letter to Vargas.

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: I'm glad you've found my citizens' missing items, Dalkar. Have you had a chance to give Vargas my letter?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet, your Majesty.

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: Oh, that's a pity.

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: Hurry up and give it to him, please. It means we can formally declare peace again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid I've lost it.

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: Very well, I'll write another. Try to take better care of this one.

sections.title: Reading the letter Queen Sigrid gives the player

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure I should be reading this...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I won't read that letter. It's for the King, not for me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure I should be reading this...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, why not. I'm the Regent, after all.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: How are your investigations going, [Fremennik name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've found the people who took the goods from your citizens!

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: Those Etceterian soldiers?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They weren't Etceterian soldiers after all.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They were teenagers from Rellekka who were trying to prove themselves as adults, by starting a war and then bringing about peace.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: Why didn't they just go through the trials as usual?

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: Unless they're the ones that the Council warned me about.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: Still, I can't believe they'd start a war between Miscellania and Etceteria, just to show they were adults...

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: But at least you've found the missing goods. Do you have them with you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid not, your Majesty. There were far too many for me to carry.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: That's a pity.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: We'll have to send people down to collect them later, then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And I also spoke to Queen Sigrid of Etceteria.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: She gave me a letter for you, which she said was a formal apology.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: Oh? Do you have this letter with you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er, I had it, but I seem to have lost it.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: You'd better ask Sigrid to write you another one, then.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: I'd like to see what she has to say.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course, your Majesty. Here you are.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: Let me see...

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: You defeated that sea snake? Sigrid seems rather impressed with that.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: And the caverns underneath her palace are joined to ours, I see.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: She thinks we should join forces to develop them for more houses.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: Finally... hmm. That's not what I'd have expected from Sigrid.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is it, your Majesty?

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: She, erm, says if I weren't a yeti, she might want to get to know me better.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: Since we're both warmongering fools, she thinks we'd get on well.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: I suppose she has a point there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Brand's suggestion worked, and I didn't even do anything.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: What was that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing, your Majesty.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you want me to give a reply to Queen Sigrid?

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: No, no. I'll think about it for a while.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: Though I'd like to tell her I'm sorry for calling her a warmongering harpy.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: In the meantime, [Fremennik name], you have managed to avert a war and bring the culprits to light.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: The kingdom of Miscellania will have to reward you for this.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: I hear that the Etceterians are also grateful for your help, and have pledged some of their resources.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: You can talk to Ghrim to deal with those, just as before.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: Thank you for your work in bringing peace back to these islands, [Fremennik name].

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: I hear that the Etceterians have also pledged to give you some of their resources, as thanks for preventing war once again.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: How is the kingdom faring?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Everything is fine, your Majesty.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: Remember, speak to Ghrim if you require assistance.

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: I hear you made peace between our kingdom and Etceteria once more. Well done, [dear/[Fremennik name]].

lines.speaker: Princess Astrid
lines.text: I hear you made peace between our kingdom and Etceteria once more. Well done, [dear/[Fremennik name]].

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: Thank you for all your help, [Fremennik name]. It's good to know that the Regent of Miscellania is willing to help his neighbours.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're welcome, your Majesty.
